subject: SIP Weekly Update - Week ending 21 Feb
deck_filename: Counter Taobao Weekly Update - Week ending 21 Feb.pdf

content.answer:
Hi Ian,

SIP weekly update for your review please. Thank you.

Performance Dashboard (as of 19 Feb)

A/ Topline/P&L


Highlights

   1.

   [Topline] Feb proj ADO 177k, 100% RR; ADO as %CB +0.23pp MoM; proj ADG
   $2.0M, 108%RR; ADG as %CB +0.34pp MoM
   1.

      Local SIP proj ADO 99% RR, ADO as % CB +0.03pp MoM; slight gap in
      target due to 1) IDMY slow recovery in 2H with low platform RR; 2) VNSIP
      prolonged Tet holiday impact
      2.

      CNSIP proj ADO 112% RR, ADO as % CB +0.10pp MoM; above target driven
      by 1) price reduction initiative in PH and VN; 2) LPP push in SG
      2.

   [P&L]
   1.

      Both Local SIP P+SA and CN SIP SA MTD daily PC2 breakeven; rest of
      Feb focus to ramp up marketing spending to maximise topline growth
      2.

      Overall combined PC2 healthy at Shopee level at $115.5k daily, +29%
      MoM. PC2 MoM growth much higher than ADG growth due to 1) ADG has
      higher base; 2) PC2 expected to be lower towards end of month to release
      more budget to marketing


B/ ADO breakdown (multiple BU/lanes)


Highlights

   1.

   IDMY proj 95% RR; Due to 1) 1H budget cut from profit passback VAT
   removal and OPEX issues; slower recovery in 2H with low platform RR. 2)
   Lower than projected Raya uplift with platform raya categories
   underachieving vs last year. Ramp up plan kicked in with 1) new SKUs ads
   push 2) high traffic SKUs product promo set up; observed improvement in ADO
   platform % by 0.16pp on D-1 vs last week
   2.

   MYSG proj 106% RR; high RR driven by successful 2.2 push, overachieving
   target with 128% R we R while ADO CB% grew 1.31pp vs 1.1 spike; expect to
   maintain with Raya assortment push
   3.

   VNMY proj 81% RR; low RR due to 1) slower Tet recovery 2) limited SLS
   rate reduction impact 3) Plt WC/MC ADO decline; GMV RR higher at 96% with
   less rebate discount vs M-1. Ramp up initiatives observed good uplift wit

*CNSIP proj ADO 112% RR, ADO as % CB +0.10pp MoM;* above target

*Overall combined PC2 healthy at Shopee level at $115.5k daily, +29%

*MYSG proj 106% RR*; high RR driven by successful 2.2 push,
*VNMY* *proj 81% RR*; low RR due to 1) slower Tet recovery 2) limited
*VN Export lanes with RR <100%*: mainly driven by the Tet holiday
*MY Import lanes with MoM +0.5 days (IDMY), +2.6 days (VNMY)*: mainly
*MYPH 119% RR, -1.8 days MoM: *outperformance mainly driven by
*CRR:* In progress of improving CRR data accuracy as it is not
*#1 (Regional Discount Tool):* UAT ongoing, to be completed ETA
*#5 (Local SIP Settlement Price): *Live release postponed by 1 week
*#6* *(FBS x SIP): *Phase 1 CB FBS x SIP scope go-live ETA end-Mar
*#8 (BRSIP):* UAT ongoing, to be completed ETA 4/Mar
Marketing solution update
SEM text ads launched to Brunei from 8 Feb, performing well with a
*SEO*: plan to raise a request to add hreflang settings for Brunei on

Hi Ian,

Slide 4



   -

   Not sure we can blame CNY? Given that there are markets who grew? Like
   SG and PH?
   -

      As discussed in the MY/SG monthly meeting, the drop in MY was led by
      top 10 MY GGPs
      -

         They contributed to 70% ADO drop in MY
         -

         Their presence is smaller in SG and thus the drop in SG was less
         impactful
         -

         They are still ramping up in PH
         -

      Additionally in PH, growth was mainly driven by key sellers and JBP
      brands, which benefited from 2.2 and 2.14 payday sale and
running trend in
      PH in Feb
      -

   On PC2, can probably benchmark it to other CB Fashion sellers, to get an
   indication on what a healthy PC2 should be?
   -

      For MY/SG, Taobao PC2 (7.4% and 6.0%) is higher than CNCB fashion PC2
      (~5%). As a next step, we will explore more investment from platform
      together with MY/SG CB team.
      -

      For other markets, we will get these figures and update on room to
      optimize in the weekly update this week



Slide 5

   -

   #1- Why is MY so much lower than SG? How about other markets?
   -

      Migration opportunity pool smaller in MY vs SG, as more SKUs had been
      in MY before the program kick-off. Please see the summary of
other markets
      below for Feb'25. [image: image.png]


   -

   #3 – Seems very slow?
   -

      We have called out the issue for Derek's attention, and there has
      been improvements in SG/MY since.
      -

      [Free ads fully expense] Target by end of month:
      -

         SG: 46/69 shops (currently 46 shops complete, vs 12 in email
         update)
         -

         MY: 63/86 shops (currently 54 shops complete, vs 30 in email
         update)
         -

      As discussed in the MY/SG CB monthly management meeting, we will
      focus on pushing 1) SKU migration; 2) seller hitting seller-level

content.question: ·  PC2 is probably too high?
How does it benchmark to SLS furniture sellers?

- For SG, Taobao furniture PC2 is 5.9% which is lower than CNCB
      furniture PC2 (7.1%).
      - For the other markets, we will update it and update in this
      Friday’s weekly



If we wish to grow this category, maybe our margins are probably too high,
both on CNCB and Taobao front..maybe we should see how we can reinvest into
the ecosystem (maybe via rate table especially on heavier items)



*From:* Nicholas Lim <nicholas.lim@shopee.com>
*Sent:* Tuesday, 25 February 2025 6:34 pm
*To:* Ian Ho <hoi@sea.com>
*Cc:* Cuiwei Sun <cuiwei.sun@shopee.com>; Jianghong Liu <
jianghong.liu@shopee.com>; Shuning Wang <shuning.wang@shopee.com>; Keljin
Chua <keljin.chua@shopee.com>; Yu Zhang <yu.zhang@shopee.com>; Ailin Xie <
ailin.xie@shopee.com>; Adeline Phoon <adeline.phoon@shopee.com>; Wei Qiang <
wei.qiang@shopee.com>; Momo Chen <momo.chen@shopee.com>; Huang Wan-Jung <
wanjung.huangwj@shopee.com>; Chiong Zhi Ann <zhiann.chiong@shopee.com>;
Steven Liu <steven.liusk@shopee.com>; Wei Loong Yong <
weiloong.yong@shopee.com>; Keith Huang <keith.huang@shopee.com>; Joe Li <
joe.liyc@shopee.com>; Johan Liew <johan.liew@shopee.com>; Ren Jiahui <
jiahui.ren@shopee.com>; Eveline Seah <eveline.seahyl@shopee.com>; Jules
Zhao <jules.zhao@shopee.com>; Tina Tai <tina.tai@shopee.com>; Tzong Meng
Ong <tzongmeng.ong@shopee.com>; Mingkit Tan <mingkit.tan@shopee.com>; Lim
Wei <wei.lim@shopee.com>
*Subject:* Re: SIP Weekly Update - Week ending 21 Feb



Hi Ian,



Slide 4



·  Not sure we can blame CNY?
Given that there are markets who grew?

Like
SG and PH?

- As discussed in the MY/SG monthly meeting, the drop in MY was led by
      top 10 MY GGPs


   - They contributed to 70% ADO drop in MY
         - Their presence is smaller in SG and thus the drop in SG was less
         impactful
         - They are still ramping up in PH


   - Additionally in PH, growth was mainly driven by key sellers and JBP
      brands, which benefited from 2.2 and 2.14 payday sale and
running trend in
      PH in Feb

·  On PC2, can probably benchmark it to other CB Fashion sellers, to get an
indication on what a healthy PC2 should be?

- For MY/SG, Taobao PC2 (7.4% and 6.0%) is higher than CNCB fashion PC2
      (~5%). As a next step, we will explore more investment from platform
      together with MY/SG CB team.
      - For other markets, we will get these figures and update on room to
      optimize in the weekly update this week



Slide 5

·  #1- Why is MY so much lower than SG?
How about other markets?

- Migration opportunity pool smaller in MY vs SG, as more SKUs had been
      in MY before the program kick-off. Please see the summary of
other markets
      below for Feb'25.

·  #3 – Seems very slow?

- We have called out the issue for Derek's attention, and there has been
      improvements in SG/MY since.
      - [Free ads fully expense] Target by end of month:


   - SG: 46/69 shops (currently 46 shops complete, vs 12 in email update)
         - MY: 63/86 shops (currently 54 shops complete, vs 30 in email
         update)


   - As discussed in the MY/SG CB monthly management meeting, we will focus
      on pushing 1) SKU migration; 2) seller hitting seller-level ADO targets,
      and push for co-investment if ADO targets not hit



·  #4 – Similar?
Seems very slow?

- Yes, we have called out this issue for Derek’s attention and we’re
      behind target
      - [Fully compliant] Target by end of the month:


   - SG: 14/69 shops (currently 6 shops complete, vs 2 in email update)
         - MY: 29/86 shops (currently 15 shops complete, vs 0 in email
         update)


   - As discussed in the MY/SG CB monthly management meeting, we will focus
      on pushing 1) SKU migration; 2) seller hitting seller-level ADO targets,
      and push for co-investment if ADO targets not hit





Slide 6

·  Only 20-35% by end Feb?
Should it not be 100%?

- 57%: supply chain fully recovered, half of them promise to complete 4
      requirements, and the other half can only complete 2 to 3 due to high
      requirements of LTD/DD/DDV nomination, expect to achieve higher compliant
      rate if MY/SG change to monthly reverse nomination from 3.15
(now 3-4 times
      per month)
      - 29%: supply chain not fully recovered or high PP, which is not
      eligible for campaign exposures
      - 14%: supply chain fully recovered but not cooperative, ok to opt
      out from the program in Mar



Slide 11

·  Not much growth/movement in general?

- Due to CNY break, many sellers reduced commitment in terms of 1) ads
      investment 2) uploading new SKUs and 3) stock replenishment
      - W-1 ads investment has recovered to ~70% of pre-CNY level
      - We expect to recover and catch up in Mar, for 1) SG: top sellers to
      upload 6k wishlist SKUs and ramp up push 2) MY: to migrate
40-50k SKUs from
      SG top sellers



·  PC2 is probably too high?
How does it benchmark to SLS furniture sellers?
